blocks.content: They walked to school the same way they always had — Daisy on the inside, Kit on the street side — but September felt different this time. Heavier. Like the air had done the math.
She smiled at the sidewalk. He watched the school come into view at the end of the block — same brick, same flag, same crooked gutter over the east entrance that had been crooked since eighth grade.
The doors opened. They went in.
